metaDescription: How to find, authorize, and manage third-party app connections in QuickBooks Online's Apps marketplace, from CRMs to payment platforms.
intro: Manually copying customer records from a CRM or duplicating e-commerce sales into QuickBooks wastes hours you don't have. QuickBooks Online's App Store lets you connect verified third-party tools directly, so data flows automatically instead of through repeated manual entry. Here's how to find, connect, and manage those integrations.

steps.heading: Open the Apps dashboard
steps.body: From the left sidebar, scroll down and click Apps to reach the integration marketplace.

steps.heading: Search the marketplace
steps.body: Click Find Apps and search for the platform you want to connect, such as Shopify, HubSpot, or Stripe.

steps.heading: Review the app listing
steps.body: Open the app's dedicated page to check ratings, sync features, then click Get App Now to begin.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Confirm the requested read-write permissions on the consent screen, then log into the external app to complete the handshake.

steps.heading: Manage active connections
steps.body: Head to the My Apps tab to monitor connected apps, pause syncs, or disconnect anything you no longer need.

faq.question: Is it safe to connect a third-party app to QuickBooks?
faq.answer: QuickBooks sandboxes third-party permissions, and you can review and adjust exactly what data access each app has been granted.

faq.question: Can I disconnect an app later?
faq.answer: Yes, the Action dropdown in My Apps lets you pause syncing or fully disconnect an integration at any time.

faq.question: Do all apps require the same setup process?
faq.answer: The general flow is similar, but each app may ask for its own specific permissions or account details during authorization.

faq.question: What if I can't find the app I'm looking for?
faq.answer: Not every tool has an official QuickBooks integration — in that case, a middleman automation platform may be needed instead.
